summary: Risk of serotonin syndrome when combined with SSRIs. Avoid this combination.
Vitamin K antagonises warfarin, reducing anticoagulant effect. Keep intake stable; do not start a high-dose vitamin K supplement without medical supervision.
Calcium reduces iron absorption when taken together. Separate doses by at least 2 hours.
B6 can reduce the effectiveness of levodopa unless taken with carbidopa.
Magnesium binds tetracyclines and reduces antibiotic absorption. Separate by 4+ hours.
High-dose fish oil may increase bleeding risk when combined with anticoagulants.
